learningObjectives: Conduct a PEO assessment to determine eligibility for foundational equipment provision by RNs, PTs, or EPs
Recognize when to escalate to OT based on integrated Person-Environment-Occupation interactions
Apply PEO framework to safely select, deliver, and train on low-risk equipment (shower chairs, over-toilet aids, bedside commodes)
Identify and avoid common novice errors in PEO application, particularly domain siloing
Document clinical reasoning, equipment setup, and patient/carer training for audit and safety

patient.name: 78-year-old patient
patient.presentingComplaint: Recent ischemic stroke with residual left-sided weakness
patient.history: Admitted 7 days ago for acute ischemic stroke affecting right MCA territory. Received acute rehab. Now medically stable, cognitively intact, able to follow commands. Requires assistance for transfers and mobility.
patient.socialHistory: Lives alone in single-storey house. Two adult children live nearby. Formerly independent in ADLs. No carer support. Smoked 20 pack-years, quit 5 years ago. Drinks alcohol socially.
patient.functionalStatus: Mobilises with single-point stick, requires minimal assistance for lower limb dressing and toileting. Independent with upper limb ADLs. Unable to transfer from bed to chair without assistance.
patient.homeEnvironment: Single-storey house, no stairs. Bathroom has step-in shower with no grab rails, narrow doorway (75cm), tiled floor, no shower chair. Toilet is standard height (45cm). Kitchen accessible.
patient.cognition: MMSE 29/30, oriented x3, understands instructions, capacity confirmed.
patient.goalsOfCare: Return home safely, maintain independence, avoid readmission.
patient.advanceCarePlan: No formal document, verbal wishes to remain at home with support if possible.

clinicalState.examination: Left hemiparesis (3/5 MRC), reduced proprioception left lower limb. Normal sensation right side. Mild dysarthria. No facial asymmetry. Normal cranial nerves. No signs of new stroke.

imaging.CT Brain: Right MCA territory infarct, no haemorrhage, no oedema
imaging.MRI Brain: Chronic white matter changes, acute right MCA infarct

investigations.functionalAssessments: TUG: 22 seconds (high fall risk), Barthel Index: 55/100 (moderate disability)

clinicalState.currentManagement: Discharge planned for tomorrow. No OT assessment scheduled. RN to assess for equipment needs prior to discharge.

constraints: No OT available until next week
Equipment loan pool has limited stock
No home modification possible before discharge

evidenceBase.guideline: Canadian Association of Occupational Therapists - PEO Framework
evidenceBase.recommendation: Assess Person, Environment, and Occupation as an integrated system — not as separate domains — to determine safe equipment provision.

evidenceBase.guideline: NSW Health - Equipment and Aids for Discharge: Clinical Guidelines
evidenceBase.recommendation: RNs, PTs, and EPs may initiate provision of foundational equipment (shower chairs, commodes, over-toilet aids) if PEO assessment indicates low risk and OT access is delayed.

evidenceBase.guideline: World Health Organization - Assistive Technology: Core Competencies for Non-Specialist Providers
evidenceBase.recommendation: Non-specialist providers must be trained to assess equipment fit, weight capacity, and user safety before provision.

evidenceBase.guideline: NHS England - Durable Medical Equipment: Provision and Safety Standards
evidenceBase.recommendation: All equipment must be fitted to the individual — standard sizes are not universally safe. Always verify transfer height and safe working load.

evidenceBase.guideline: Australian Commission on Safety and Quality in Health Care - Safe Patient Handling and Movement
evidenceBase.recommendation: Never exceed equipment SWL. Always ensure patient can bear weight through legs and push up with arms during transfers.

evidenceBase.guideline: Journal of Allied Health - Non-OT Provision of Mobility Equipment: A Systematic Review
evidenceBase.recommendation: Non-OT provision of foundational equipment reduces falls and readmissions when paired with structured PEO assessment and patient training.

evidenceBase.guideline: EnableNSW - Equipment Loan Scheme: Operational Manual
evidenceBase.recommendation: Equipment must be delivered, set up, and trained on by a qualified clinician prior to discharge. Documentation of setup and training is mandatory.

evidenceBase.guideline: Invacare - Bedside Commode Installation and Weight Capacity Guide
evidenceBase.recommendation: Ensure commode height matches patient’s current toilet height. Feet must rest flat on floor. SWL must exceed patient weight by 20%.
